tweets.text:
The S&P 500 keeps making 1-year highs while more stocks fall than rise. May triggered 5 separate breadth divergence days, the most in records back to 1998. Year-to-date count: 9.

Price up. Participation down. https://t.co/QCPlheFcHd

tweets.text: Good Morning from Germany, where citizenship numbers have hit a new record: 332,500 foreigners became German citizens in 2025, up 14% from 2024. Syrians remained the largest group, but naturalizations of Turkish and Russian nationals jumped 51% each; likely helped by Germany’s new dual-citizenship rules.
tweets.full_text: Good Morning from Germany, where citizenship numbers have hit a new record: 332,500 foreigners became German citizens in 2025, up 14% from 2024. Syrians remained the largest group, but naturalizations of Turkish and Russian nationals jumped 51% each; likely helped by Germany’s new dual-citizenship rules.

thread_tweets.text:
After Friday's jobs report, remember: 

There are 135 million private sector jobs.   

25% of the labor market is recession-proof.  

Another 59% barely moves.  

Only 15.5% actually drives the cycle.  

This chart shows the full breakdown, ranked by cyclicality: https://t.co/X3lPamAB44

thread_tweets.text:

Twitter is where I test sentiment, test to see what comes back. Thank you for being a rat in the experiment.

Substack is where I actually do my analysis, it is detailed, and it is frequent.

If you had read the analysis, you'd know how incorrect your comment is.

tweets.text:
@extropic_vc @PeterLBrandt @TechCharts Hint: my analysis isn't on X.

Whatever you think you know about my work, is quite obviously incomplete.

Truthfully, and respectfully, I have no interest in your measuring contest. I have my receipts, twice a week.

All the very best.
